facts.fact: The insurer is ValueGuard Life Insurance Company.
The policy number is VG-ROP-2024-4183.
The policy issue date is September 15, 2024.
The policy type is 20-Year Return of Premium Term Life.
The insured's name is Nicole Christine Martinez.
Nicole Christine Martinez's date of birth is April 7, 1988.
Nicole Christine Martinez's gender is Female.
Nicole Christine Martinez's age at issue is 36.
Nicole Christine Martinez's smoker status is Non-Smoker.
Nicole Christine Martinez's underwriting rating is Preferred Plus.
The death benefit is $300,000.
The policy term is 20 years, ending September 15, 2044.
The annual premium is $1,620.
The premium mode is Annual.
The return of premium at end of term is $32,400 (if policy maintained).
The primary beneficiary is Carlos Roberto Martinez (Husband) at 100%.
The contingent beneficiary Sofia Isabella Martinez (Daughter) receives 50%.
The contingent beneficiary Marco Daniel Martinez (Son) receives 50%.
This is a Return of Premium (ROP) Term Life Insurance Policy.
If the insured survives the 20-year term and all premiums have been paid, the insurer will return 100% of all premiums paid ($32,400) to the policy owner.
The policy combines life insurance protection during the term, return of all premiums if the insured outlives the term, and an option to convert to permanent insurance.
If the insured dies during the policy term while the policy is in force, the insurer will pay $300,000 to the beneficiary.
Required claim documents include a certified death certificate.
Required claim documents include a completed claim form.
Required claim documents include government-issued ID.
If the insured is alive on September 15, 2044, and all premiums have been paid, the insurer will pay the policy owner $32,400 (100% of premiums paid over 20 years).
If the policy is surrendered in Years 1-5, 0% of premiums paid are returned.
If the policy is surrendered in Years 6-10, 30% of premiums paid are returned.
If the policy is surrendered in Years 11-15, 60% of premiums paid are returned.
If the policy is surrendered in Years 16-19, 90% of premiums paid are returned.
If the policy is surrendered in Year 20, 100% of premiums paid are returned.
If death occurs during the term, beneficiaries receive the full death benefit ($300,000) PLUS a proportional premium refund based on the early surrender schedule.
If death occurs in Year 8, beneficiaries receive $300,000 + (30% x $12,960 premiums paid) = $303,888.
ROP payments are made within 60 days of the applicable date.
The annual premium of $1,620 is due each September 15.
The premium is level and will not increase during the term.
To receive the full return of premium at end of term, ALL premiums must be paid on time.
The grace period is 31 days; if premium is not paid within grace period, the policy lapses and the return of premium benefit is forfeited.
Missed premiums cannot be made up.
If a premium is paid late (during the grace period), the ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment.
The policy owner may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance policy without evidence of insurability.
Conversion is available at any time before the policy expires, or before the insured's 65th birthday, whichever is earlier.
The minimum conversion amount is $50,000.
The maximum conversion amount is $300,000 (current face amount).
Upon conversion, the new policy premium is based on attained age.
Upon conversion, the ROP benefit is forfeited.
Upon conversion, premiums paid to date are NOT returned.
Policy loans are NOT available under this policy type.
Partial withdrawals are NOT permitted under this policy type.
The only way to access value is through full surrender (with applicable ROP percentage) or waiting until end of term for full ROP payment.
If the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of the issue date, the insurer will pay only the premiums paid without interest; no death benefit is payable.
The insurer may contest the policy within 2 years of issue if there was material misrepresentation in the application.
No war exclusion applies to this policy.
No aviation exclusion applies to this policy.
No travel exclusion applies to this policy.
No hazardous activity exclusion applies to this policy.
If the policy lapses, the owner may apply for reinstatement within 5 years.
Reinstatement requires satisfactory evidence of insurability.
Reinstatement requires paying all past due premiums with interest at 6% per annum.
Reinstatement requires the insured to still be alive.
Upon reinstatement, the ROP benefit is recalculated to exclude the lapsed period.
A new 2-year suicide and contestability period begins upon reinstatement.
This policy costs more than standard term life insurance because of the return of premium feature.
The ROP benefit is NOT considered a taxable gain when paid (per policy notes; tax advisor should be consulted).
This policy does NOT build cash value that can be borrowed against during the term.
If age was misstated, the insurer will adjust the death benefit to what the premium would have purchased at the correct age; the ROP amount is not affected.
As policy owner, the insured may change beneficiaries at any time.
As policy owner, the insured may convert to permanent insurance.
As policy owner, the insured may assign the policy as collateral.
As policy owner, the insured may surrender the policy for the applicable ROP percentage.
